parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: Read this email from Mrs Silva, the organiser of your school sports day, and the notes you have made.

stimulus.head: EMAIL
stimulus.body:
From: Mrs Silva
Subject: School Sports Day — next Friday

Dear Students,

Our Sports Day is next Friday! You can take part in running, football, basketball or volleyball. Which event would you like to enter?

We also need some students to help serve drinks and put up posters. Can you help with anything?

Mrs Silva

bullets: say which event you would like to enter and why
offer to help with drinks or posters
suggest a way to make Sports Day more fun
ask one question about the day
tasks.write: Write your email to Mrs Silva.

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write your answer in about 100 words.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement in your school English-language magazine.

stimulus.title: THE SPORT I LOVE WATCHING
stimulus.body: Some people love playing sport, but watching it can be just as exciting! Which sport do you most enjoy watching, and why? Write an article and tell us. The best articles will be published next month.

bullets: where and how you usually watch it

tasks.lead: Your English teacher has asked you to write a story.
tasks.startSentence: With one minute left in the match, Leo finally got his chance.

stimulus.body:
Your story must begin with this sentence:

With one minute left in the match, Leo finally got his chance.
